title: List Specs
description: Agents and CLI users need a single query to discover what specs exist across all workspaces without loading full spec content. The `ListSpecs` use case enumerates all specs across all configured workspaces, returning a title for each entry and optionally a short summary and metadata freshness status. It supports optional workspace filtering so callers can restrict results to one or more named workspaces. It is the primary query for discovery UIs and CLI listing commands.
context: Agents and CLI users need a single query to discover what specs exist across all workspaces without loading full spec content. The `ListSpecs` use case enumerates all specs across all configured workspaces, returning a title for each entry and optionally a short summary and metadata freshness status. It supports optional workspace filtering so callers can restrict results to one or more named workspaces. It is the primary query for discovery UIs and CLI listing commands.

rules:
`ListSpecs.execute(options?)` SHALL obtain the orchestrated project structure via the `ListWorkspaces` use case (or the corresponding kernel capability). It SHALL iterate through the resulting `ProjectWorkspace` entities and call `SpecRepository.list(options)` on each workspace's `specRepo`.

It MUST forward the same `ListOptions`, `includeSummary`, and `includeMetadataStatus` to every workspace repository call. It MUST NOT re-sort, re-filter, or re-paginate per-workspace results after the repository returns.

The merged result MUST preserve workspace declaration order from `ListWorkspaces`, with each workspace's items in the repository's canonical path order. Cross-workspace pagination is out of scope for v1 — callers paginate within a single workspace via the port or filter workspaces explicitly.

Workspace filtering SHALL be performed by matching workspace names against the orchestrated list before invoking `list()`.

rules.requirement: Always resolve a title for each entry
rules: Every returned `SpecListEntry` MUST include a `title` field supplied by `SpecRepository.list()`. The use case MUST NOT perform additional metadata or file reads to resolve titles when the repository already returned them.

When `options.includeSummary` is `true`, the use case MUST forward the flag to `SpecRepository.list()` and project `summary` onto merged results only when the repository included it in the cached entry.

The use case MUST NOT re-resolve summary via `SpecRepository.metadata()`, `spec.md` reads, or `extractSpecSummary` when the repository already returned a summary for that entry.

When `includeSummary` is `false` or omitted, `summary` MUST NOT appear on any entry.

When `options.includeMetadataStatus` is `true`, the use case MUST forward the flag to `SpecRepository.list()` and project `metadataStatus` only when the repository included it in the cached entry.

The use case MUST NOT re-compute metadata freshness via `SpecRepository.metadata()`, content hashing, or schema validation when the repository already returned `metadataStatus`.

When `includeMetadataStatus` is `false` or omitted, `metadataStatus` MUST NOT appear on any entry.

rules.requirement: Silent error handling for metadata and summary reads
rules:
Per-spec title/summary/status resolution errors are handled at index materialization time in `FsSpecIndexCache`. `ListSpecs` MUST NOT perform supplementary I/O that could throw for individual specs.

The use case SHALL NOT propagate I/O errors from optional field projection to the caller when merging repository list results.

Each entry MUST include required fields `workspace`, `path`, and `title` as returned by `SpecRepository.list()`.

Optional fields (`summary`, `metadataStatus`) MUST only be present when explicitly requested via include flags **and** projected from the repository result.

When workspace filtering is active, the result array contains entries only from the filtered workspaces.

The config-based `createListSpecs(config, options?)` form MUST derive `ListSpecsDeps` through `resolveListSpecsDeps(resolver)` and then delegate to canonical `createListSpecs(deps)`.

`resolveListSpecsDeps(resolver)` MUST resolve:

- `listWorkspaces: ListWorkspaces`

It MUST NOT resolve `hasher: ContentHasher` or `yaml: YamlSerializer` — `ListSpecs` orchestrates workspace repositories and forwards list options; title/summary/metadata-status enrichment happens inside `SpecRepository.list()`'s index materialization, not in this use case, so it has no dependency on content hashing or YAML serialization.

The helper is the only use-case-specific composition entry for config-based bootstrap. The factory MUST NOT reconstruct fs-shaped wiring inline.

constraints: The use case MUST NOT modify the repositories.
It SHALL depend on ListWorkspaces for consistent project traversal.
It MUST NOT re-resolve title, summary, or metadata status with extra I/O when the repository already returned those fields.
Include flags are forwarded to repositories for projection-only filtering of cached payloads.

scenarios.name: Multiple workspaces returned in declaration order
given: two workspaces alpha and beta configured in that order, each containing specs
when: execute() is called with no options
then: all entries from alpha appear before all entries from beta
AND each workspace's items remain in repository canonical path order

scenarios.name: Empty workspace included without error
then: the result array contains no entries for that workspace and no error is thrown

scenarios.name: Workspace filter limits results
given: specs exist in workspaces alpha and beta
then: only entries from workspace alpha are returned

scenarios.name: ListSpecs forwards include flags to each repository
when: execute({ includeSummary: true, includeMetadataStatus: true, limit: 50 }) is called
then: each workspace SpecRepository.list() receives the same forwarded options
AND the use case does not re-sort or re-paginate per-workspace results

scenarios.requirement: Always resolve a title for each entry
scenarios.name: Title supplied by repository list
given: SpecRepository.list() returns entries with resolved titles
then: each entry's title matches the repository-provided value
AND the use case does not perform additional metadata or file reads to resolve titles

scenarios.requirement: Always resolve a title for each entry
scenarios.name: Title fallback comes from repository index materialization
given: a spec at path auth/login indexed without metadata title
then: the entry's title is "login" as returned by SpecRepository.list()
AND ListSpecs does not read metadata or spec files to derive the title

scenarios.requirement: Always resolve a title for each entry
scenarios.name: Empty metadata title fallback comes from repository
given: a spec indexed with empty trimmed metadata title and path auth/login
then: the entry's title is "login" from the repository result

scenarios.name: Summary forwarded from repository when requested
given: SpecRepository.list({ includeSummary: true }) returns entries with cached summary
then: merged entries include the repository-provided summary

scenarios.name: Summary omitted when not requested
then: no entry has a summary property

scenarios.name: Use case does not re-resolve summary with extra I/O
given: the repository already returned a cached summary for a spec entry
then: the use case does not call metadata(), read spec.md, or invoke summary extraction helpers for that entry

scenarios.name: metadataStatus forwarded from repository when requested
given: SpecRepository.list({ includeMetadataStatus: true }) returns entries with cached metadataStatus
then: merged entries include the repository-provided metadataStatus

scenarios.name: Status not present when not requested
then: no entry has a metadataStatus property

scenarios.name: Use case does not re-compute freshness with extra I/O
given: the repository already returned metadataStatus for a spec entry
then: the use case does not call metadata(), content hashing, or schema validation for that entry

scenarios.requirement: Silent error handling for metadata and summary reads
scenarios.name: Repository swallows per-spec resolution errors at index time
given: a spec whose title/summary/status resolution fails during index materialization
then: the entry still appears with repository-provided fallback fields
AND no error is thrown to the caller

scenarios.requirement: Silent error handling for metadata and summary reads
scenarios.name: ListSpecs does not perform supplementary I/O for optional fields
when: execute({ includeSummary: true, includeMetadataStatus: true }) is called
then: the use case merges repository list results without additional per-spec file reads beyond repository delegation

scenarios.name: Entry contains required fields from repository
then: each entry contains workspace, path, and title as returned by SpecRepository.list()

scenarios.name: Optional fields appear only when requested and projected
when: execute({ includeSummary: true, includeMetadataStatus: true }) is called
then: entries may contain summary and metadataStatus only when the repository projected them

scenarios.name: Workspace filter limits merged results
then: the result array contains entries only from workspace alpha

scenarios.name: createListSpecs config form derives ListSpecsDeps through resolveListSpecsDeps
then: it creates a composition resolver for that composition session
AND it derives ListSpecsDeps through resolveListSpecsDeps(resolver)
AND resolveListSpecsDeps(resolver) resolves:
AND the factory delegates to canonical createListSpecs(deps)
